Title: Caffeoylquinic acids: chemistry, biosynthesis, occurrence, analytical challenges, and bioactivity

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway feeding caffeoylquinic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: QDH can be used to enhance quinic acid precursor supply for caffeoylquinic acid (CQA) engineering, but should likely be combined with downstream phenylpropanoid/CQA-pathway genes because precursor increase alone may not ensure high CQA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly reduces 3-dehydroquinic acid to quinic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the quinic acid pool that serves as the acyl acceptor backbone for CQA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis and pathway assignment summarized from prior studies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states QDH is structurally similar to SDH and appears confined to seed plants, suggesting evolution from ancestral SDH.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Quinic acid branch of shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QDH produces quinic acid in the shikimate pathway. 2. Quinic acid is a direct structural precursor for CQA biosynthesis. 3. QDH is evolutionarily distinct from the universally conserved SDH and is reported to be confined to seed plants.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vacuole; leaves; flowers; seeds; fruits and other plant organs depending on species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant specialized metabolite with antioxidant and defense-related roles and major dietary polyphenol beneficial for human health

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid pathway feeding caffeoylquinic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: IbPAL1 overexpression can raise chlorogenic acid/CQA levels, but deployment requires spatiotemporal control or tissue-specific expression because constitutive elevation can alter lignification and inhibit storage-root formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of IbPAL1 increased chlorogenic acid levels in sweet potato leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in sweet potato with metabolite measurement and growth phenotype observation.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream; committed entry into phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review also notes PAL generally converts phenylalanine to p-coumaric acid together with C4H in the canonical route.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stimulated secondary xylem expansion in stems and inhibited storage-root formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbPAL1 overexpression increases chlorogenic acid/CQA accumulation in sweet potato leaves. 2. PAL feeds carbon into the phenylpropanoid pathway for CQA biosynthesis. 3. Elevated flux causes developmental tradeoffs, highlighting the need for precise engineering control.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 3-O-caffeoylquinic acid (chlorogenic acid) and related caffeoylquinic acids

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 4CL is useful for boosting caffeoyl-CoA precursor formation but should be balanced with HQT/HCT and lignin-related branch enzymes to avoid undesirable flux into lignin or other phenolics.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids such as p-coumaric acid and caffeic acid to their CoA thioesters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces p-coumaroyl-CoA and caffeoyl-CoA, central activated intermediates for CQA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction summarized from prior biochemical and genetic studies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In route 1, caffeic acid released by CSE is reconverted to caffeoyl-CoA by 4CL before transfer to quinic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux competition with flavonoid, stilbene, and lignin monomer biosynthesis is likely.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL generates activated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA intermediates required for CQA synthesis. 2. It connects the general phenylpropanoid pathway to both quinic acid and shikimic acid shunts. 3. Because the same intermediates feed lignin and flavonoid pathways, 4CL affects metabolic flux partitioning.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid pathway; shikimate shunt and quinate shunt
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: HCT can be engineered to redirect hydroxycinnamoyl flux toward mono- and possibly di-CQAs, but caution is needed because HCT is tightly coupled to lignin biosynthesis and may cause structural/growth phenotypes if strongly perturbed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups between CoA esters and shikimic acid or quinic acid; can also catalyze reverse reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates p-coumaroyl/caffeoyl shikimate or quinates and, in some species, can also support 3,5-diCQA synthesis from 3-CQA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical characterization and prior gene suppression/heterologous expression studies summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and immediate precursor; can also contribute to diCQA formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low vacuolar pH favored related acyltransferase activity for diCQA formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coffea spp. HCT was reported to catalyze not only normal 3-CQA synthesis but also 3,5-diCQA production from 3-CQA.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shikimic acid shunt; quinic acid shunt
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely affects lignin biosynthesis because the same shikimate esters are intermediates in monolignol formation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: p-Coumaroyl shikimate; p-coumaroyl quinate; caffeoyl-CoA or caffeoyl/quinate esters depending on reaction direction
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: p-Coumaroyl-CoA or caffeoyl-CoA with shikimic acid/quinic acid; or corresponding esters in reverse reaction
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; vacuole-associated activity discussed for diCQA context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups to shikimate or quinate intermediates central to CQA biosynthesis. 2. It can operate in both forward and reverse directions, linking multiple CQA routes. 3. In some species it also contributes to 3,5-diCQA formation, but its strong linkage to lignin metabolism creates engineering constraints.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and health-promoting dietary specialized metabolites

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Solanum lycopersicum; Cynara cardunculus var. scolymus; Solanum tuberosum; Lonicera japonica
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: HQT is a strong engineering target to elevate 3-CQA and, in some contexts, 3,5-diCQA. Gene stacking with precursor-supply genes may be effective, but tissue specificity and vacuolar/cytosolic context should be considered to limit pleiotropic effects on lignin/flavonoid networks.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA to quinic acid; can also catalyze reverse and secondary acyltransferase reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major determinant of 3-CQA accumulation; in tomato HQT also produces 3,5-diCQA from 3-CQA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene suppression analyses in tomato and artichoke; spatiotemporal expression-metabolite correlation in Lonicera japonica; biochemical characterization summarized from prior studies.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product; can also form higher-order CQA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low pH/vacuolar environment implicated in 3,5-diCQA synthesis in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Spatiotemporal HQT expression directly correlates with 3-CQA biosynthesis sites in Lonicera japonica flowers; HQT levels are associated with 3-CQA production in tomato, artichoke, and potato.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High where 3-CQA accumulates, especially floral tissues in Lonicera japonica
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream to downstream; branch point toward mono- and di-CQAs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato HQT can synthesize 3,5-diCQA by using either 3-CQA plus caffeoyl-CoA or two 3-CQA molecules. Review notes cytosolic/chloroplastic synthesis of 3-CQA followed by vacuolar storage.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because the pathway is linked to lignin/flavonoid metabolism, altered HQT flux may perturb other phenylpropanoids; explicit penalties not detailed for HQT suppression/overexpression here.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeoyl-CoA and quinic acid; or 3-CQA in secondary acyltransferase reactions
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Solanum lycopersicum; Cynara cardunculus var. scolymus; Solanum tuberosum; Lonicera japonica
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gene suppression in tomato and artichoke showed HQT plays a major role in 3-CQA production from caffeoyl-CoA. 2. HQT expression correlates with 3-CQA biosynthesis sites in Lonicera japonica flowers. 3. Tomato HQT can also generate 3,5-diCQA, making it a central flux-control enzyme for both mono- and di-CQAs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major dietary polyphenols with antioxidant and health-promoting activities

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: C3'H/CYP98 is a candidate for increasing caffeoyl ester formation, but because it is deeply integrated with lignin biosynthesis, manipulations should be moderate or tissue-specific.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates p-coumaroyl shikimate or p-coumaroyl quinate at the 3' position of the aromatic ring to form the corresponding caffeoyl esters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces caffeoyl shikimate or caffeoyl quinate, directly enabling 3-CQA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Established biochemical pathway assignment summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor or direct product depending on route
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review distinguishes this cytochrome P450-type ester-specific 3'-hydroxylase from the newer peroxidase-type C3H discovered in Brachypodium.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shikimate and quinate ester hydroxylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely impacts lignin monomer synthesis due to shared intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3'H/CYP98 hydroxylates p-coumaroyl esters to caffeoyl esters. 2. This reaction directly enables canonical 3-CQA formation in the quinic acid shunt. 3. It is a central shared step between lignin-related phenylpropanoid metabolism and CQA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Medicago spp.; Populus spp.; Larix kaempferi
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CSE status helps predict which CQA route a species uses. In CSE-containing species, pathway engineering should consider the route through caffeic acid and caffeoyl-CoA; in CSE-lacking species alternative routes or C3H may be more relevant.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes caffeoyl shikimate to caffeic acid and shikimic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Channels caffeoyl shikimate into route 1 for 3-CQA synthesis via caffeic acid and then caffeoyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene conservation/suppression evidence summarized from previous studies across angiosperms and gymnosperms.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states CSE is conserved in most plants, but absent in some species such as Brachypodium distachyon and Zea mays, implying route variation among taxa.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shikimate shunt to caffeic acid route
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong linkage to lignin pathway suggests possible structural phenotypes upon manipulation, though not detailed here.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Medicago spp.; Populus spp.; Larix kaempferi
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSE hydrolyzes caffeoyl shikimate to caffeic acid, supporting route 1 to 3-CQA. 2. The gene is conserved in most plants examined. 3. Absence of CSE in some grasses implies that those species rely on alternative CQA biosynthetic routes.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Peroxidase-type C3H offers an alternative engineering solution for CSE-lacking grasses to enhance caffeic acid and downstream CQA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly hydroxylates p-coumaric acid to caffeic acid via a peroxidase-type mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables an alternative route to 3-CQA formation via free caffeic acid in Brachypodium distachyon.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Discovery and enzymatic/pathway characterization summarized from prior study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as a newly discovered route particularly relevant to species lacking CSE, such as Brachypodium distachyon and possibly other grasses.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A novel peroxidase-type C3H was discovered in Brachypodium distachyon. 2. It directly converts p-coumaric acid to caffeic acid. 3. This provides a new 3-CQA biosynthetic route especially relevant in CSE-lacking grasses.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Sweet potato; Nicotiana benthamiana; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ipomoea batatas; Nicotiana benthamiana; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: IblCS is a strong target for metabolic engineering or biomanufacturing of 3,5-diCQA because it has higher catalytic efficiency than sweet potato HCT for this product and functioned effectively in heterologous yeast production.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a caffeoyl group between two 3-CQA molecules to form 3,5-diCQA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major catalyst for 3,5-diCQA synthesis in sweet potato; heterologous expression enabled efficient 3,5-diCQA production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme characterization of recombinant protein produced in Nicotiana benthamiana; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris with product quantification.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Optimum reaction conditions pH 6.3 and 39.9 degrees C were reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as a GDSL lipase-like enzyme. Km for 3-CQA was 3.5 mM. Pichia pastoris expressing IblCS produced 1.2 g/L 3,5-diCQA from 2.6 g/L 3-CQA with 61.3% molar efficiency. Related family members were noted in Rauvolfia serpentina and Alopecurus myosuroides.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for 3,5-diCQA in sweet potato
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IblCS is a newly identified GDSL lipase-like isochlorogenic acid synthase from sweet potato. 2. It directly converts two 3-CQA molecules into 3,5-diCQA and likely plays a major role in this branch. 3. The enzyme outperforms sweet potato HCT for 3,5-diCQA production and supports efficient heterologous bioproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Sweet potato tissues; vacuolar accumulation is discussed more generally for CQAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive dietary polyphenol with antioxidant and neuroprotective potential
